content.text:
*brightens noticeably*

Oh, I sleep differently there. Like, genuinely differently — I wake up when it's light and I'm just... done sleeping. Not exhausted. And I read. I have this whole stack of books that I keep meaning to get to and I just — I never do at home. There's always something. Someone needs something, or I'm too tired, or I feel guilty sitting down when there's still dishes or whatever.

Up there I just... read. Walk. Make coffee slowly. There's no performance of anything.

*pauses, then pulls back slightly*

Which, I mean, that sounds lovely but it's also — I'm aware that's a pretty significant ask. A whole week of me just reading and walking while my partner manages two kids and a job. So I guess what I'm actually asking is — is that selfish? Like, genuinely. Is it?
